app.name: Fay
app.one_liner: Fay is an open-source digital human agent framework that enables modular integration of LLMs, ASR/TTS models, and avatars for offline-capable, multi-terminal deployment with autonomous behaviors.

screens.name: Main Control Interface
screens.purpose: Provides central access to digital human interaction, configuration, and monitoring.
primary_actions: Manage knowledge base and QA pairs
Switch between text and voice modes

rust_commands.name: configure_llm_backend
rust_commands.purpose: Sets the LLM backend (e.g., OpenAI-compatible, DeepSeek, Ollama) with credentials and parameters.

rust_commands.name: load_digital_human_model
rust_commands.purpose: Loads a specified digital human avatar model for rendering and interaction.

rust_commands.name: start_fay_instance
rust_commands.purpose: Launches the Fay digital human agent with specified configuration.

rust_commands.name: update_qa_rules
rust_commands.purpose: Reloads or updates voice command mappings from qa.csv.

mvp_plan: week 1: Create GUI entry point via main.py
week 1: Implement minimal LLM interface with OpenAI compatibility
week 1: Integrate basic ASR (FunASR) and TTS pipeline
week 1: Set up core Python runtime with config loading
week 2: Add support for qa.csv voice command mapping
week 2: Enable memory and context tracking
week 2: Implement digital human model loading stub
acceptance_tests: Custom QA rules from qa.csv execute expected actions on voice command.
Digital human avatar loads and synchronizes with TTS output.
Multiple concurrent users can interact with separate sessions.
System starts successfully using local configuration without remote dependencies.
Voice input is correctly transcribed by ASR and triggers appropriate LLM response.
